servinfo.title: Inspection of ECM and Its Circuits

item.ptxt: As each terminal voltage is affected by battery voltage, confirm that it is 11 V or more when ignition is “ON”.
Voltage with asterisk (*) cannot be measured with voltmeter because it is pulse signal. Use oscilloscope for its check.
Before performing this inspection, be sure to read “Precautions for ECM Circuit Inspection” under Precautions for Engine .

ptxt.intxref: Fuel injector No.4 signal and ignition coil No.4 signal

entry.ptxt: CAN (high) communication circuit to TCM (A/T model)

ptxt.intxref: Fuel injector No.1 signal and ignition coil No.1 signal

Fuel injector No.4 signal and ignition coil No.4 signal
CKP sensor signal and CMP sensor signal

Fuel injector No.1 signal and ignition coil No.1 signal

entry.ptxt: CAN (low) communication circuit to TCM (A/T model)

entry.ptxt: MAF and IAT sensor and ECT sensor ground circuit

ptxt.intxref: Fuel injector No.3 signal and ignition coil No.3 signal
Fuel injector No.2 signal and ignition coil No.2 signal

entry.ptxt: EVAP canister purge valve drive circuit
CAN (high) communication circuit to ABS / ESP® control module
CAN (low) communication circuit to ABS / ESP® control module
A/C refrigerant pressure sensor ground circuit
Transmission range sensor (Keyless push start-A/T model) or CPP switch No.1 (Keyless push start-M/T model) signal circuit
Select lever: “P” or “N” (Keyless push start-A/T model)
Clutch pedal: Fully depressed (Keyless push start-M/T model)
Select lever: Other than “P” or “N” position (Keyless push start-A/T model)
Clutch pedal: Not depressed (Keyless push start-M/T model)
A/C refrigerant pressure sensor power supply circuit
A/C refrigerant pressure sensor signal circuit
Radiator cooling fan relay No.2 and No.3 drive circuit
Radiator cooling fan relay No.1 drive circuit

item.ptxt: How to read waveforms is shown below.
Waveform may vary with measurement condition and vehicle specifications.
Engine: Idle speed after warming up

Engine: Stop
Ignition: “ON”
